name: Clip
classification_notes: Clip is explicitly described as a modular typeface intended primarily as a headline face, with a distinctiveness so extreme the designers note it seems built for a single application — hallmarks of display-category type.
summary: A modular headline typeface revived by Asger Behncke Jacobsen in 2015 and completed with Mads Wildgaard in 2017, available only on request.
description: Built from modular construction principles, Clip is so singularly distinctive that the designers themselves note it appears suited to just one specific application. The revival began in 2015 under Asger Behncke Jacobsen and was completed in collaboration with Mads Wildgaard by 2017. It covers a broad range of European languages and is distributed in OTF and WOFF2 formats, though it is not available for standard purchase.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions describe Clip as a modular typeface revived by Asger Behncke Jacobsen in 2015 and completed with Mads Wildgaard in 2017. They note it is a highly distinctive headline typeface, so specific in character that it almost seems designed with a single application in mind. It covers a majority of European languages and is available only on request.

name: Glossy Display
classification_notes: The family name and page context suggest a display-oriented sans with extended and italic variants, positioned for headline and expressive use rather than body text.
summary: A bold, extended display sans from Bold Decisions, offered in a single weight with italic.
description: Glossy Display is a bold-weight display typeface with an extended width variant and italic cut. Its positioning suggests use in headline and campaign contexts where width and presence are priorities. The limited weight range keeps it focused rather than versatile.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Glossy Display as a bold extended display typeface with an italic variant, designed for expressive typographic use.

name: Gc-16
classification_notes: The minimal page content and name suggest an experimental or display-oriented typeface; the presence of only Regular and Italic styles, alongside the coded naming convention, points to a niche display or experimental category.
summary: A two-style experimental typeface from Bold Decisions, offered in Regular and Italic.
description: Released by Bold Decisions under a coded alphanumeric name, Gc-16 is a minimal offering comprising a Regular and Italic. The naming convention is consistent with Bold Decisions' broader typographic practice of treating typefaces as discrete design objects rather than extensive families. Beyond its two styles, little additional information is available from the page.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions lists Gc-16 with a Regular and Italic style, presenting it in the stripped-back manner typical of the foundry's catalogue.

name: Lars
classification_notes: The page lists Roman as a style alongside Regular and Extended, suggesting a serif typeface with width variants; classified as transitional serif pending more detail from the minimal page content.
summary: A serif typeface from Bold Decisions available in regular and extended widths, with a Roman cut.
description: Lars is a serif from Bold Decisions offered in at least two widths, Regular and Extended, with a Roman style. The page provides minimal detail, but the combination of width variants and the Roman designation points toward a text-oriented serif family. Its scope and intended use case remain difficult to assess from the available copy.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions list Lars with Regular, Extended and Roman cuts.

name: Glossy Magazine
classification_notes: The name and the three-style family structure suggest a high-contrast display serif aimed at editorial and fashion contexts, consistent with the 'glossy magazine' aesthetic the name invokes.
summary: A three-style display serif from Bold Decisions pitched at editorial and fashion publishing, offered in Bold, Standard and Italic.
description: With only three named styles, Glossy Magazine keeps its scope narrow: Bold, Standard and Italic cover the core editorial range without additional weights or widths. The name signals its intended territory clearly, pointing toward magazine layouts, fashion branding and headline work where a high-contrast serif personality is expected. The page offers little additional detail about its origins or designer.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present Glossy Magazine as a typeface available in Bold, Standard and Italic cuts.

name: Lars Cyrillic
classification_notes: The page references weights and styles typical of a grotesque sans-serif family, and the Cyrillic suffix indicates a script extension of an existing Latin grotesque. The naming and foundry context suggest a contemporary grotesque lineage.
summary: A Cyrillic extension of Bold Decisions' Lars grotesque, available in a medium weight with extended and italic variants.
description: Lars Cyrillic brings Cyrillic script support to the Lars family from Bold Decisions. The page lists a medium weight alongside extended and italic cuts, suggesting a focused but practical range for multilingual typesetting. The extension follows the grotesque character of the original Latin family.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Lars Cyrillic as a Cyrillic-script complement to their Lars typeface, providing medium, extended, and italic styles for use across Latin and Cyrillic languages.

name: Lars Greek
classification_notes: The page lists weights and a condensed variant consistent with a grotesque sans-serif structure, and the name references Lars, a known grotesque family from Bold Decisions.
summary: A Greek-script extension of the Lars grotesque family, available in Thin and Roman weights with a condensed cut.
description: Lars Greek brings Greek-language support to the Lars grotesque, a sans-serif family from Bold Decisions. The available styles include Thin and Roman, with a condensed variant offering tighter setting options. Suited to multilingual projects requiring consistent typographic treatment across Latin and Greek scripts.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Lars Greek as a Greek-script companion to their Lars family, with Thin and Roman weights alongside a condensed style.

name: Lars Mono Cyrillic
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with Cyrillic script support, classified as mono given the explicit naming and foundry categorisation.
summary: A monospaced type family from Bold Decisions with Cyrillic support, available in Regular and Italic.
description: Lars Mono Cyrillic extends monospaced typesetting into Cyrillic script territory, offering a Regular and Italic pair. The Cyrillic-specific release suggests the family is built for multilingual environments where fixed-width type is required. Its scope is intentionally minimal, prioritising utility over decorative ambition.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Lars Mono Cyrillic in Regular and Italic, a monospaced typeface supporting Cyrillic script.

name: Lars Mono
classification_notes: Described simply as Regular and Roman, this is a monospaced typeface with a roman upright cut, consistent with a minimal mono release from an independent foundry.
summary: A single-weight monospaced roman from Bold Decisions, offered in one upright cut without italics or weight variants.
description: Lars Mono is a stripped-back monospaced typeface from Bold Decisions, available in a single Regular roman style. With no italic, no weight range and no condensed cuts, it reads as a deliberate exercise in restraint. The page offers little further detail, but the minimalist approach suggests a typeface designed for focused, functional use.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present Lars Mono with a single Regular roman style, offering a minimal monospaced typeface with no additional weights or variants listed.

name: Lars Rounded
classification_notes: The name and foundry URL path confirm a rounded variant of a sans-serif typeface; rounded terminals are the defining subcategory trait.
summary: A rounded, extended sans-serif from Bold Decisions, offering a soft geometric character suited to display and branding use.
description: Lars Rounded is an extended rounded sans from Bold Decisions, built around soft terminals that give it a friendly, open quality distinct from more utilitarian grotesques. The extended width positions it for headlines, packaging and identity work where generous spacing is an asset. Page content is sparse, so precise weight range and language coverage remain unclear from available data.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present Lars Rounded as a rounded sans available in an extended roman style.

name: Lars Mono Greek
classification_notes: The name explicitly identifies this as a monospaced typeface with Greek script support, placing it firmly in the mono category.
summary: A thin-weight monospaced typeface from Bold Decisions with Greek script support.
description: Lars Mono Greek extends monospaced type into Greek script territory, offered here in a single Thin Roman weight. The combination of a monospaced structure with Greek coverage suits technical and multilingual contexts where consistent character spacing is required. Bold Decisions present it as part of the broader Lars family.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Lars Mono Greek as a monospaced typeface supporting Greek script, available in a Thin Roman style.

name: Reklame
classification_notes: The name 'Reklame' (German for advertising/commercial) and the single weight suggest a display-oriented grotesque in the tradition of commercial lettering; classified as sans-serif grotesque based on context and naming conventions.
summary: A single-weight grotesque from Bold Decisions, taking its name from the German word for advertising.
description: Reklame draws on the tradition of commercial and advertising lettering, as suggested by its German-derived name. Only a Regular weight is listed, pointing to a focused, display-oriented release rather than a broad text family. Bold Decisions is a small independent foundry, and Reklame fits their stripped-back, deliberate approach to type releases.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions offer Reklame as a single Regular weight typeface, with the name referencing the German word for advertising or commercial print.

name: Tradition
classification_notes: The page labels the typeface simply as 'Roman', indicating a classical serif design in the roman tradition, though insufficient detail is available to specify further.
summary: A roman serif from Bold Decisions with minimal published detail beyond its classification as Roman.
description: Listed under the name Tradition, this typeface is described only as Roman on the Bold Decisions catalogue page, leaving weight range, language support and year of release undocumented. The name suggests a classical or historically informed approach to roman letter forms. Without further specimen or copy, specific design intent and intended use cases remain unclear.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions list Tradition with the single classification of Roman, providing no additional descriptive copy on the typeface page.

name: Spread
classification_notes: The single weight 'Extra Bold' and the display context implied by the foundry's page suggest a heavy, attention-grabbing display sans rather than a text typeface.
summary: A single Extra Bold weight from Bold Decisions, built for high-impact display and poster use.
description: Spread offers one weight — Extra Bold — pointing squarely at headline and poster applications where mass and presence matter more than range. The page yields little additional detail, but the name and weight suggest a wide or spread typographic stance. Bold Decisions is a small independent foundry, and Spread reads as a focused, single-purpose release.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions lists Spread as an Extra Bold typeface available through their foundry.

name: True Sans
classification_notes: The page references 'Roman' and 'Extended' variants, and the foundry name 'Bold Decisions' with minimal copy suggests a stripped-back grotesque or geometric sans; classified as grotesque based on available naming conventions.
summary: A bold extended sans from Bold Decisions with very limited published information available on the typeface page.
description: True Sans appears to offer at least a Bold Extended Roman cut, suggesting a display-oriented grotesque with width variation. The minimal page content makes it difficult to assess the full family or its design intentions. What little is present points to a no-frills sans aimed at graphic applications.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present True Sans with a Bold Extended Roman variant, with little additional copy provided on the typeface page.

name: True Serif
classification_notes: The family offers Book and Italic weights only, suggesting a text-focused serif designed for sustained reading rather than display use.
summary: A minimal two-style serif from Bold Decisions, offering a single Book weight with matching Italic for text-setting work.
description: True Serif is stripped back to essentials: a Book weight and its Italic, with no additional weights cluttering the family. The restraint suggests a typeface conceived for body copy and sustained reading rather than headline or display applications. Little additional information is available from the page, making it difficult to assess the full scope of the design.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present True Serif with a Book and Italic, offering a focused, minimal serif family.

name: True Sans Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced variant within the True Sans family, categorised as mono due to its fixed-width character spacing and sans-serif letterforms.
summary: A monospaced sans companion to the True Sans family, available in at least a Thin weight.
description: True Sans Mono is the fixed-width variant of Bold Decisions' True Sans family, designed for contexts requiring uniform character spacing such as code or tabular data. The page indicates a Thin weight, though the full weight range is not fully documented in the available copy. Published by Berlin-based foundry Bold Decisions.
foundry_blurb: Bold Decisions present True Sans Mono as part of the True Sans type system, offering a monospaced variant suited to technical and screen-based applications.
